title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
summary: Cursor's Sam Whitmore, Baseten's Charlie O'Neill and Harry Partridge discuss the future of agent orchestration, focusing on scaling to hundreds of parallel coding agents, inter-agent communication and review mechanisms, and building integrated systems rather than managing isolated concurrent tasks.
writeup: Cursor's Sam Whitmore talks with Baseten's Charlie O'Neill and Harry Partridge about orchestrating coding agents at scale, up to 128 running in parallel. The conversation covers inter-agent communication and review mechanisms, plus the shift from managing isolated concurrent tasks toward building integrated multi-agent systems. The framing connects agent orchestration and test-time compute as the levers for scaling to hundreds of parallel agents.
